examples.title: Basic sigsuspend() Example - Waiting for Child
examples.description: Demonstrates using sigsuspend() to wait for child process termination with proper signal mask management
examples.explanation: This program shows proper use of sigsuspend(). First, SIGCHLD is blocked using sigprocmask(). After forking, the parent uses sigsuspend() with an empty mask to wait for the child. When SIGCHLD arrives (unblocked in waitmask), sigsuspend() returns, allowing waitpid() to collect the status. Finally, the original mask is restored.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is time until child terminates

examples.title: sigsuspend() with Critical Section Protection
examples.description: Shows how to protect a critical section using sigsuspend() while allowing a specific signal
examples.explanation: This demonstrates critical section protection. The key pattern is checking the flag BEFORE calling sigsuspend(), which prevents the lost wakeup problem. If flag is not set, sigsuspend() atomically changes the mask (allowing SIGUSR1) and waits. When SIGUSR1 arrives, the handler sets flag=1 and sigsuspend() returns.

examples.title: General Pattern: sigsuspend() Usage Template
examples.description: Generic pseudocode showing the standard pattern for using sigsuspend() safely
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the universal pattern for using sigsuspend() correctly. The key is the atomic operation: sigsuspend() changes the mask and waits in one step, preventing any signal from being missed. Always save the original mask and restore it after use.
